messages.content: Hello! How may I assist you?
Yes, I can understand and speak Moroccan Darija! How may I assist you in Moroccan Darija?
Sure, I'd be happy to help! What do you need translated?
How can I say “I like money more than anything else”
In Moroccan Darija, you can say: "Kanbghik tdir awal men kolchi huwa lmouneet".
Translation of I like money in Standard Moroccan Darija
The translation of "I like money" in Standard Moroccan Darija would be "kanhibik lmoun" (or "kanhebbik lmoun" in some dialects).
"Kat3jebni" means "you impress me" or "you make me happy" in Moroccan Darija.
I like Money in Standard Darija
The phrase "I like money" in Standard Moroccan Arabic (Darija) is "Kanheb lflous" or "Kanhib lflous" (in some dialects).
"I like" in Moroccan Darija is "Kanbghik" (informal) or "Kanbghikum" (formal or addressing more than one person).
